name: Modal
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Modal's design language — a serverless-Python-for-AI platform built on a stark white canvas with a signature warm orange (#FFB949 mustard / #FF7A30 ember) accent, a grid that loves to expose its own dividers, and a 'literal command' aesthetic where the hero IS a code block running real Python.

design_language.motion_signature: Refuses motion almost entirely — 150ms ease-out on hover, signature is the live-running code block on the homepage that types Python characters at ~30 chars/sec then logs a tensor.

trademark_signals: Warm mustard primary (#FFB949) — most-recognized brand cue, never a saturated orange, never a brand yellow
Exposed grid: vertical 1px dividers run from header to footer on the homepage, slicing the page into a 12-column grid you can see — the brand is comfortable showing its skeleton
Live Python code block as hero — the homepage runs a real Modal function (text-to-image, transcription, batch GPU inference) inside the hero, typing real Python characters in monospace
Square or near-square geometry (0px or 4px corners) — the brand refuses pill buttons
Inter at every tier with IBM Plex Mono for code — refuses any serif
ai_slop_avoided: Don't paint the mustard as a saturated orange or a McDonald's yellow — the specific #FFB949 is the brand
Don't round buttons to pills. Modal refuses the AI-category soft geometry
Don't substitute a faux-code mockup for the hero — the live running code IS the brand
Don't introduce a second accent. The mustard is the entire chromatic system; ember is a sub-accent only on the largest CTAs
Don't hide the exposed grid dividers — they ARE the visual signature
